objective: Service-to-service authentication and authorization is enforced for every microservice call (no implicit trust between services).
tsa: Design mutual TLS (mTLS) or service-mesh (Istio/Linkerd/App Mesh) identity between services; enforce JWT/OAuth2 token validation at each service boundary; zero-trust network segmentation between namespaces/VPC subnets.
grc: Verify a documented service-trust model exists and maps to the organization's Zero Trust / segmentation policy; confirm exceptions (services still on implicit trust) are risk-accepted and tracked.
owasp: OWASP API Security Top 10 (API2:2023 Broken Authentication); OWASP ASVS V2 (Authentication)
cis: CIS Kubernetes Benchmark 5.3 (Network Policies); CIS Controls v8 - Control 12 (Network Infrastructure Mgmt)
aws: AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar - SEC03/SEC05 (Identity, Network Protection); App Mesh / VPC Lattice mTLS guidance
automation: Service mesh policy-as-code (Istio AuthorizationPolicy), automated mTLS certificate rotation, OPA/Gatekeeper admission control
tod: Review service mesh / API gateway architecture diagrams and mTLS configuration standards; confirm design mandates authentication at every hop, not only at the perimeter.
toi: Inspect live mesh configuration (Istio PeerAuthentication, AuthorizationPolicy or equivalent) on a sample of namespaces; confirm mTLS mode is STRICT, not PERMISSIVE, in production.
toe: Over a defined period, sample access/audit logs and mesh telemetry to confirm no plaintext or unauthenticated inter-service calls were permitted in production.
advisory: Advise on target-state service mesh architecture, phased migration from PERMISSIVE to STRICT mTLS, and a segmentation model aligned to data classification.
assurance: Independently test a sample of production namespaces/services for the actual enforced mTLS/authZ posture and report exceptions with risk ratings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.20-A.8.23; NIST CSF PR.AC/PR.DS
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Microservices follow the principle of least privilege for inter-service data access and secrets scoping.
tsa: Define per-service IAM roles/service accounts scoped to only the resources that service needs; avoid shared service accounts; use short-lived tokens (IRSA/Workload Identity) instead of static keys.
grc: Confirm a RACI/ownership matrix ties each microservice to a named owner and a documented least-privilege role; check periodic entitlement review cadence.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A01:2021 (Broken Access Control); OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Access Control
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 6 (Access Control Management)
aws: AWS IAM Best Practices - least privilege, IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA), Access Analyzer
automation: AWS IAM Access Analyzer, automated unused-permission detection, policy-as-code (Cedar/OPA) validation in CI
tod: Review the IAM role design standard for microservices and confirm least-privilege and no-shared-credential principles are documented as mandatory.
toi: Sample a set of live service accounts/IAM roles and confirm attached policies match the documented minimum required permissions.
toe: Review IAM Access Analyzer findings and CloudTrail/K8s audit logs over a period to confirm no service exceeded its scoped permissions and unused permissions were remediated.
advisory: Recommend a role-scoping taxonomy and an entitlement-review workflow; help remediate over-privileged legacy service accounts.
assurance: Test a sample of service accounts against Access Analyzer output and flag unremediated excess-privilege findings as audit exceptions.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.20-A.8.23; NIST CSF PR.AC/PR.DS
evidence: IAM/role configuration exports, Access Analyzer findings, and recertification sign-off records.

objective: Microservice API contracts and inter-service traffic are inventoried, versioned, and monitored for unauthorized new endpoints (shadow/zombie APIs).
tsa: Maintain a live service catalog / API registry integrated with the API gateway; enforce schema (OpenAPI/AsyncAPI) validation at deploy time; alert on undocumented endpoints detected in traffic.
grc: Confirm an authoritative, current API/service inventory exists and is reconciled against what is actually deployed; verify a process to retire orphaned services.
owasp: OWASP API Security Top 10 (API9:2023 Improper Inventory Management)
mitre: T1590 (Gather Victim Network Information), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 1/2 (Inventory of Assets & Software)
aws: AWS API Gateway usage plans and API Gateway access logging; AWS Well-Architected SEC03
automation: API discovery tooling (e.g., traffic-based shadow API detection), CI schema-diff gates, automated service catalog sync
tod: Review the process/tooling used to maintain the service and API inventory and confirm it is a mandated, owned control (not ad hoc).
toi: Reconcile the documented inventory against a live scan of deployed services/namespaces for a sample environment; identify gaps.
toe: Over the period, review reconciliation records/exception logs to confirm shadow or orphaned services were detected and remediated in line with SLA.
advisory: Design the inventory reconciliation cadence and tooling integration (CMDB <-> service mesh/API gateway) and define retirement criteria.
assurance: Independently scan the environment and compare against the inventory of record; report undocumented or orphaned services as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.20-A.8.23; NIST CSF PR.AC/PR.DS
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: CI/CD pipelines enforce mandatory security gates (SAST, SCA, secrets scan, DAST) before promotion to higher environments.
tsa: Embed SAST (e.g., Semgrep/SonarQube), SCA (e.g., Snyk/Dependabot), secrets scanning (e.g., gitleaks/TruffleHog) and DAST as blocking pipeline stages; configure severity thresholds that fail the build.
grc: Confirm a pipeline security-gate policy is formally approved, documents which scans are mandatory per environment, and defines override/exception authority.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline; OWASP Top 10 A06:2021 (Vulnerable and Outdated Components); OWASP ASVS V14 (Configuration)
mitre: T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1195.001 (Compromise Software Dependencies)
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide; CIS Controls v8 - Control 16 (Application Software Security)
aws: AWS CodePipeline/CodeBuild security best practices; Amazon Inspector for CI artifacts
automation: Pipeline-as-code gates (fail-the-build thresholds), automated ticket creation for findings, dashboard aggregation (DefectDojo)
tod: Review pipeline-as-code templates (Jenkinsfile/YAML) to confirm security scanning stages exist, are mandatory (not optional/manual), and cannot be bypassed without documented approval.
toi: Select a sample of pipeline runs and confirm the scan stages actually executed, produced results, and enforced the configured thresholds.
toe: Over a period, review pipeline execution logs and override/exception approvals to confirm gates were consistently enforced and bypasses were properly authorized and documented.
advisory: Design the target pipeline security-gate architecture, severity/threshold policy, and exception workflow; assist tool selection and integration.
assurance: Test a sample of pipeline runs (including any that bypassed gates) against the approved policy and report unauthorized bypasses as control failures.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Build and deployment credentials/secrets used by CI/CD runners are stored in a managed secrets vault and never embedded in pipeline code or environment variables in plaintext.
tsa: Integrate pipelines with a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager/HashiCorp Vault); use short-lived, dynamically generated credentials (OIDC federation for CI to cloud, e.g., GitHub Actions OIDC to AWS IAM) instead of long-lived static keys.
grc: Confirm a policy prohibits static credentials in pipeline definitions/repos; verify a periodic secrets-rotation and static-credential-elimination target is tracked.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A02:2021 (Cryptographic Failures); OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Secrets Management
mitre: T1552.001 (Credentials In Files), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 3 (Data Protection); CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 1.x (IAM key rotation)
aws: AWS Secrets Manager, IAM OIDC identity federation for CI/CD, KMS envelope encryption
automation: Automated secrets-scanning (pre-commit + pipeline), automated static-key expiry/rotation, OIDC federation removing long-lived keys
tod: Review the secrets-management standard for pipelines and confirm it mandates a vault/OIDC federation and prohibits static long-lived credentials.
toi: Sample pipeline configurations/repos and confirm no plaintext secrets or long-lived static credentials are present; confirm vault/OIDC integration is actually configured.
toe: Review secrets-scanning tool alerts and credential-rotation logs over a period to confirm continuous compliance and timely remediation of any exposed secret.
advisory: Recommend migration path from static keys to OIDC federation/dynamic secrets and design the rotation and break-glass process.
assurance: Run/independently review secrets-scanning results across repositories and pipelines and report any live exposed credentials as high-risk findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Build artifacts are signed and their provenance is verifiable before deployment (SLSA-aligned build integrity).
tsa: Implement artifact signing (Sigstore/Cosign, in-toto attestations) and generate SLSA provenance metadata; enforce signature verification as an admission-control gate in the deployment target (e.g., Kubernetes admission webhook).
grc: Confirm a policy requires provenance/signature verification prior to deployment and defines the target SLSA maturity level for the organization.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Artifact Integrity; aligns to SLSA framework (industry-recognized, referenced alongside OWASP guidance)
mitre: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1554 (Compromise Client Software Binary)
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide - Build Integrity
aws: AWS Signer, ECR image scanning and immutable tags, CodeBuild provenance
automation: Automated Cosign/Sigstore signing in pipeline, admission-controller signature verification (Kyverno/Connaisseur), SBOM generation on build
tod: Review the build-integrity standard to confirm signing and provenance requirements are documented and mapped to a defined SLSA target level.
toi: Select a sample of deployed artifacts/images and confirm each carries a valid signature/attestation and that deployment tooling actually enforces verification.
toe: Over the period, review admission-controller logs/deployment records to confirm unsigned or provenance-failed artifacts were consistently blocked.
advisory: Advise on SLSA maturity roadmap, signing tool selection, and key-management/HSM design for the signing keys themselves.
assurance: Independently verify a sample of production artifacts' signatures/provenance and report any deployed artifact lacking valid attestation.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Secure coding standards (input validation, output encoding, safe error handling) are defined, mandatory, and enforced via static analysis.
tsa: Adopt OWASP ASVS as the baseline secure coding standard per language/framework; integrate SAST rulesets mapped to ASVS controls; provide secure code libraries/snippets to reduce reinvention of controls.
grc: Confirm a formally approved secure coding standard exists, is version-controlled, mapped to OWASP ASVS levels appropriate to data sensitivity, and developers attest to it.
owasp: OWASP ASVS (all chapters, esp. V5 Validation/Sanitization, V7 Error Handling); OWASP Top 10 A03:2021 (Injection)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 16 (Application Software Security)
aws: AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar SEC11 (Application Security)
automation: SAST integrated in IDE and pipeline, pre-commit linting hooks, automated ASVS-control-coverage reporting
tod: Review the secure coding standard document, confirm ASVS mapping, approval, and version history; confirm it is referenced in the SDLC policy.
toi: Select a sample of recently merged code changes and confirm SAST evidence exists and findings above threshold were remediated before merge.
toe: Over a period, review SAST trend data/defect-density metrics to confirm sustained adherence and timely remediation of recurring classes of findings.
advisory: Help define/refresh the secure coding standard, select SAST rulesets, and design a developer secure-coding training/certification path.
assurance: Test a sample of code changes and pipeline SAST evidence against the approved standard and report non-conformances as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; OWASP ASVS (referenced standard)
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Third-party/open-source components used in code are inventoried, licensed appropriately, and free of known critical vulnerabilities before release.
tsa: Implement SCA tooling generating a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM, CycloneDX/SPDX) per build; block builds with critical/high CVEs lacking an approved exception.
grc: Confirm an open-source usage/license policy exists, SBOM generation is mandatory, and an exception/waiver process with expiry dates is enforced.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A06:2021 (Vulnerable and Outdated Components); OWASP Dependency-Check project
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide
aws: Amazon Inspector (SCA scanning for Lambda/ECR), AWS CodeGuru
automation: Automated SBOM generation per build, dependency-update bots (Dependabot/Renovate), CVE-threshold pipeline gates
tod: Review the open-source/SCA policy to confirm mandatory SBOM generation and defined CVE severity thresholds with an exception process.
toi: Sample recent builds and confirm SBOMs were generated, reviewed, and no unresolved critical CVEs shipped without an approved, time-bound exception.
toe: Over the period, track exception expiry and remediation SLAs to confirm expired exceptions were not left open and re-scans occurred.
advisory: Advise on SBOM tooling/format selection, exception governance workflow, and integration with vulnerability management.
assurance: Independently review SBOM and SCA scan history for a sample of releases and report expired/unresolved critical findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; OWASP ASVS (referenced standard)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Peer code review is mandatory for all changes, including a security-focused review checklist for sensitive modules (auth, crypto, payments).
tsa: Configure branch protection rules requiring minimum reviewer count and CODEOWNERS approval for sensitive paths; provide a security review checklist template integrated into the PR process.
grc: Confirm a documented change-management/SDLC policy mandates independent code review and segregation of duties (author cannot self-approve/merge).
owasp: OWASP ASVS V1 (Architecture) - secure SDLC; OWASP Code Review Guide
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 16.11 (Peer Review)
aws: AWS CodeCommit/GitHub branch protection integration with CodePipeline approval actions
automation: Branch protection enforced in repo settings, automated CODEOWNERS routing, blocked self-merge via platform policy
tod: Review branch-protection configuration standards and the SDLC policy to confirm mandatory independent review and segregation of duties are required.
toi: Sample a set of merged pull requests and confirm each has an independent approver, no self-merges occurred, and the security checklist was completed for sensitive modules.
toe: Over the period, review repository audit logs for branch-protection overrides/admin bypasses and confirm none occurred without documented, approved exception.
advisory: Help design branch-protection standards, CODEOWNERS structure, and a risk-based review checklist for high-sensitivity code paths.
assurance: Test a sample of merges for segregation-of-duties compliance and report self-merges or bypassed reviews as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; OWASP ASVS (referenced standard)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: IaC templates (Terraform/CloudFormation/Bicep) are statically scanned for misconfigurations before provisioning, and drift from approved templates is detected.
tsa: Integrate IaC scanners (Checkov, tfsec, cfn-nag) into the pipeline as blocking gates; implement drift-detection (Terraform Cloud/AWS Config) comparing live state to source of truth.
grc: Confirm a policy mandates IaC scanning prior to apply and defines acceptable misconfiguration severity thresholds and drift-remediation SLAs.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Infrastructure Security
mitre: T1195.003 (Compromise Software Supply Chain - infra), T1578 (Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure)
cis: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark; CIS Controls v8 - Control 4 (Secure Configuration)
aws: AWS Config Rules, AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar SEC01 (Account/Infra Foundations)
automation: Policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel/Checkov) pipeline gates, AWS Config conformance packs, automated drift alerts
tod: Review the IaC governance standard to confirm mandatory scanning, defined severity gates, and a drift-detection/remediation SLA.
toi: Sample a set of recent IaC change sets/pull requests and confirm scan evidence exists and critical findings were remediated before apply.
toe: Over the period, review AWS Config/drift-detection reports to confirm live infrastructure remained aligned with approved IaC and drift was remediated within SLA.
advisory: Recommend IaC scanning tool selection, policy-as-code rule authoring, and a drift-remediation operating model.
assurance: Independently compare a sample of live infrastructure state against source-controlled IaC and report unauthorized drift as a finding.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP; RBI/SEBI cloud outsourcing guidelines
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: IaC state files and modules are stored securely with access controls and encryption; sensitive values are never hardcoded in templates.
tsa: Store Terraform state in encrypted remote backends (S3 with SSE-KMS + state locking via DynamoDB) with restricted IAM access; reference secrets from a vault via data sources, never as literals.
grc: Confirm a standard requires encrypted, access-controlled remote state storage and prohibits hardcoded secrets in IaC repositories.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A02:2021 (Cryptographic Failures)
cis: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 2.x (S3/KMS encryption controls)
aws: Amazon S3 SSE-KMS, DynamoDB state locking, AWS Well-Architected SEC08 (Data Protection at Rest)
automation: Automated secrets-scan of IaC repos, state-file encryption enforced via bucket policy, access logging on state bucket
tod: Review the state-management standard confirming encryption, locking, and access-control requirements for remote state.
toi: Inspect the actual state backend configuration and IAM policy for a sample environment to confirm encryption and least-privileged access are implemented.
toe: Review access logs to the state bucket/backend over a period to confirm only authorized pipeline identities accessed state, with no anomalies.
advisory: Advise on secure remote-state architecture, key management for state encryption, and secrets-referencing patterns.
assurance: Test the state backend configuration and access logs against policy and report unencrypted state or excessive access as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP; RBI/SEBI cloud outsourcing guidelines
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: IaC modules are version-controlled, peer-reviewed, and sourced only from an approved internal module registry (no unvetted public modules in production).
tsa: Stand up a private Terraform/CloudFormation module registry; pin module versions with checksums; restrict pipeline network egress to prevent pulling unvetted public registries in production builds.
grc: Confirm a policy restricts production IaC to approved, vetted modules and requires version pinning; verify a vetting process exists for new modules.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Supply Chain
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide
aws: AWS Service Catalog, CodeArtifact for private module hosting
automation: Private registry enforcement via network policy, automated module-version pinning checks, CI egress allow-listing
tod: Review the module-sourcing policy confirming mandatory use of the vetted private registry and version pinning.
toi: Sample production IaC configurations and confirm all referenced modules originate from the approved registry with pinned, checksum-verified versions.
toe: Over the period, review pipeline egress logs/module-source audits to confirm no unvetted public modules were pulled into production builds.
advisory: Help stand up the private module registry, define the module-vetting workflow, and design version-pinning conventions.
assurance: Independently audit a sample of production IaC for unvetted or unpinned module sources and report exceptions.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP; RBI/SEBI cloud outsourcing guidelines
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Container images are built from minimal, hardened base images, scanned for vulnerabilities, and only images meeting policy are admitted to the cluster.
tsa: Use distroless/minimal base images; integrate image scanning (Trivy/ECR scan/Clair) in the pipeline and at registry level; enforce an admission controller (Kyverno/OPA Gatekeeper) blocking unscanned or non-compliant images.
grc: Confirm a container image standard mandates minimal base images and scanning, with defined severity thresholds enforced at admission.
owasp: OWASP Docker/Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheets; OWASP Top 10 A06:2021
cis: CIS Docker Benchmark; CIS Kubernetes Benchmark 5.1 (Image Provenance)
aws: Amazon ECR image scanning, EKS best practices guide (image security)
automation: Registry-integrated scanning on push, admission-controller policy enforcement, automated base-image patch pipelines
tod: Review the container image security standard confirming minimal base image and scanning-at-admission requirements.
toi: Sample a set of running pods/images in production and confirm each passed scanning and admission-policy checks with no unresolved critical CVEs.
toe: Over the period, review admission-controller denial logs and registry scan history to confirm consistent enforcement and remediation of newly disclosed CVEs.
advisory: Advise on base-image standardization, scanning tool integration, and admission-policy design (Kyverno/Gatekeeper policy authoring).
assurance: Independently scan a sample of running images and compare against admission logs; report images running with unresolved critical vulnerabilities.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9, A.8.20; NIST CSF PR.PT
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Kubernetes workloads run with least-privilege security contexts (non-root, read-only root filesystem, no privileged containers) and network policies restrict pod-to-pod traffic by default.
tsa: Enforce Pod Security Standards (restricted profile) via admission control; set securityContext (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities); implement default-deny NetworkPolicies with explicit allow rules.
grc: Confirm a hardening baseline (mapped to CIS Kubernetes Benchmark) is formally adopted and default-deny network segmentation is a mandated design principle.
owasp: OWASP Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheet
mitre: T1611 (Escape to Host), T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation)
cis: CIS Kubernetes Benchmark 4.2 (Pod Security), 5.3 (Network Policies)
aws: Amazon EKS Best Practices Guide - Pod Security Standards, Security Groups for Pods
automation: Kyverno/OPA Gatekeeper policies enforcing Pod Security Standards, automated NetworkPolicy templating per namespace
tod: Review the Kubernetes hardening baseline document and confirm it maps to CIS Kubernetes Benchmark and mandates default-deny network policy.
toi: Sample a set of namespaces/workloads and confirm security contexts and NetworkPolicies are actually applied and match the baseline.
toe: Over the period, run/review CIS Kubernetes Benchmark compliance scan results (kube-bench or equivalent) to confirm sustained conformance.
advisory: Help author the Pod Security Standard policies, default NetworkPolicy templates, and a namespace-onboarding hardening checklist.
assurance: Independently run a CIS Kubernetes Benchmark scan against a sample of clusters/namespaces and report deviations as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9, A.8.20; NIST CSF PR.PT
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Kubernetes cluster control-plane and etcd access is restricted, authenticated via RBAC, and audit logging is enabled and forwarded to a central SIEM.
tsa: Enable Kubernetes audit logging with an appropriate policy; encrypt etcd at rest; enforce RBAC least-privilege for cluster-admin and namespace roles; integrate with managed control plane (EKS) logging to CloudWatch/SIEM.
grc: Confirm cluster access-control (RBAC) design is documented, cluster-admin is restricted to a minimal named group, and audit logs are retained per the log-retention policy.
owasp: OWASP Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheet - RBAC
mitre: T1078.004 (Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts), T1552.007 (Container API)
cis: CIS Kubernetes Benchmark 1.x (Control Plane), 3.2 (Logging)
aws: Amazon EKS control plane logging, IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA), CloudTrail integration
automation: Automated RBAC-review scripts (rbac-lookup/kubeaudit), audit-log forwarding pipeline to SIEM, alerting on cluster-admin usage
tod: Review the cluster RBAC design and audit-logging standard confirming least-privilege roles and mandatory log forwarding.
toi: Inspect live RBAC bindings for a sample cluster and confirm cluster-admin is restricted; confirm audit logs are actively flowing to the SIEM.
toe: Over the period, review SIEM alerts/audit-log completeness reports to confirm continuous logging and appropriate use of privileged cluster roles.
advisory: Advise on RBAC role design, audit-policy tuning to balance signal/noise, and SIEM integration architecture for K8s audit events.
assurance: Independently review RBAC bindings and audit-log continuity for a sample of clusters and report excessive privilege or logging gaps.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9, A.8.20; NIST CSF PR.PT
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: System and application configurations are managed as code (Ansible/Chef/Puppet/SSM) with an approved secure baseline and no manual, undocumented configuration changes.
tsa: Define configuration-as-code baselines aligned to CIS Benchmarks per OS/application; use configuration management tooling to enforce and auto-remediate drift; disable/restrict direct console/SSH configuration changes in production.
grc: Confirm a configuration management policy requires baseline definition, change-controlled updates, and prohibits manual out-of-band changes to production systems.
owasp: OWASP ASVS V14 (Configuration)
mitre: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable Security Tools)
cis: CIS Benchmarks (per OS/platform); CIS Controls v8 - Control 4 (Secure Configuration Management)
aws: AWS Systems Manager (State Manager, Config), AWS Config conformance packs
automation: Automated configuration drift detection and remediation (SSM State Manager, Chef InSpec), scheduled CIS benchmark compliance scans
tod: Review the configuration management policy confirming baseline standards, change control, and prohibition of manual production changes.
toi: Sample a set of production systems and confirm their actual configuration matches the approved baseline (via CIS-CAT/InSpec scan).
toe: Over the period, review drift-detection and remediation logs to confirm out-of-baseline changes were detected and corrected within SLA.
advisory: Help define baselines per platform, select configuration-management tooling, and design the drift-remediation workflow.
assurance: Independently run a baseline compliance scan against a sample of systems and report deviations and unremediated drift as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP-1; CIS Controls v8
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: All configuration changes follow a formal change-management process with risk classification, approval, and rollback plan (aligned to ITIL Change Enablement).
tsa: Integrate change requests (ServiceNow/Jira) with pipeline deployment gates so production changes cannot deploy without a linked, approved change record; automate rollback via pipeline versioned artifacts.
grc: Confirm the Change Management policy (ITIL-aligned) defines standard/normal/emergency change categories, approval authority, and CAB cadence; confirm emergency changes are retrospectively reviewed.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to ITIL v4/ITSM Change Enablement practice
mitre: T1078 (Valid Accounts) - unauthorized change as initial foothold vector
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 4.1 (Secure Configuration Process)
aws: AWS CodePipeline manual-approval actions gated to change records
automation: Pipeline integration blocking deploy without an approved CR reference, automated CAB reporting, auto-generated rollback artifacts
tod: Review the Change Management policy/procedure confirming categorization, approval workflow, CAB governance, and emergency-change review requirements.
toi: Sample a set of production deployments and confirm each is linked to an approved, appropriately categorized change record.
toe: Over the period, review the change log/CAB minutes to confirm all changes were authorized, emergency changes were retrospectively reviewed, and unauthorized changes (if any) were investigated.
advisory: Advise on integrating ITSM change tooling with the CI/CD pipeline and designing risk-based change categorization criteria.
assurance: Test a sample of production deployments for change-record linkage and approval evidence; report unauthorized or unapproved changes as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP-1; CIS Controls v8
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Golden/hardened images (AMIs, container base images, VM templates) are maintained centrally, versioned, and refreshed on a defined patch cadence.
tsa: Build golden images via an image pipeline (EC2 Image Builder/Packer) from a hardened CIS-aligned base, rebuild on a scheduled cadence, and deprecate old AMI/image versions automatically.
grc: Confirm a golden-image lifecycle policy defines ownership, refresh cadence, and deprecation criteria, and that only approved golden images are permitted for new deployments.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Infrastructure Hardening
cis: CIS Benchmarks for base OS; CIS Controls v8 - Control 4
aws: AWS EC2 Image Builder, AMI lifecycle management, AWS Well-Architected SEC01
automation: Scheduled image-pipeline rebuilds, automated AMI deprecation/deregistration, image-provenance tagging
tod: Review the golden-image lifecycle policy confirming refresh cadence, ownership, and mandatory use for new deployments.
toi: Sample a set of recently launched instances/images and confirm they originate from a current, approved golden image version.
toe: Over the period, review image-pipeline build history and deprecation logs to confirm the refresh cadence was met and stale images were retired.
advisory: Help design the image pipeline, refresh cadence, and deprecation automation aligned to patch SLAs.
assurance: Independently sample running instances against the approved golden-image registry and report use of deprecated or unapproved images.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.9; NIST CSF PR.IP-1; CIS Controls v8
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Repository access is role-based, MFA-enforced, and repositories are classified so that sensitive/production repos have tighter controls than sandbox/experimental repos.
tsa: Enforce SSO+MFA for repository access (GitHub Enterprise/GitLab/CodeCommit); implement repository classification tagging with branch protection tiers escalating for production-linked repos.
grc: Confirm an access-governance policy for repositories defines classification tiers, MFA mandate, and a periodic access-recertification process.
owasp: OWASP ASVS V2 (Authentication)
mitre: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 6 (Access Control Management)
aws: AWS CodeCommit IAM policies, SSO integration via IAM Identity Center
automation: SSO/MFA enforcement at IdP level, automated quarterly access-recertification workflow, repo-classification tagging in code
tod: Review the repository access-governance policy confirming classification tiers, MFA enforcement, and recertification cadence are documented.
toi: Sample a set of repositories and confirm access lists match approved roles, MFA is enforced, and classification tags are correctly applied.
toe: Over the period, review access-recertification records and authentication logs to confirm MFA was consistently enforced and stale access was removed.
advisory: Advise on repository classification scheme, SSO/MFA rollout, and recertification workflow design.
assurance: Independently test a sample of repositories for access-list accuracy, MFA enforcement, and timely removal of stale access; report exceptions.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4, A.5.9; NIST CSF PR.AC
evidence: IAM/role configuration exports, Access Analyzer findings, and recertification sign-off records.

objective: Commit signing is enforced and commit history/audit trail is immutable and monitored for force-push or history-rewrite events.
tsa: Require GPG/Sigstore commit signing; disable or tightly restrict force-push on protected branches; enable repository audit-log streaming to a SIEM.
grc: Confirm a policy requires signed commits on protected branches and prohibits force-push/history rewrite without documented exception approval.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Source Code Integrity
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide - Source Code Management
aws: AWS CodeCommit approval rule templates and event notifications via EventBridge
automation: Branch-protection settings blocking force-push, automated commit-signature verification checks, SIEM alerting on rewrite events
tod: Review the source-code integrity policy confirming mandatory commit signing and force-push restrictions on protected branches.
toi: Sample protected branches and confirm force-push is disabled, and inspect a sample of commits for valid signatures.
toe: Over the period, review repository audit logs/SIEM alerts for any force-push or history-rewrite events and confirm each was investigated and authorized.
advisory: Help configure branch-protection and signing enforcement and design SIEM alert rules for anomalous repository events.
assurance: Independently review repository audit logs for unauthorized force-pushes/history rewrites and report as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4, A.5.9; NIST CSF PR.AC
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: Repositories are continuously scanned for exposed secrets, and a defined incident response runbook triggers automatically upon detection.
tsa: Deploy repo-wide secrets scanning (gitleaks/GitHub Advanced Security push protection) with pre-receive hooks blocking commits containing secret patterns; auto-trigger credential revocation workflow upon detection.
grc: Confirm an incident-response runbook exists specifically for exposed-secret events, with defined revocation SLA and post-incident review requirement.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A02:2021 (Cryptographic Failures)/A05:2021 (Security Misconfiguration)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 3 (Data Protection)
aws: AWS Secrets Manager auto-rotation triggered by detection, GuardDuty for anomalous credential-use detection
automation: Pre-receive/push-protection hooks, automated secret revocation Lambda/webhook, SIEM correlation with credential-use anomalies
tod: Review the exposed-secret incident runbook confirming detection, automatic containment/revocation steps, and post-incident review requirements are defined.
toi: Select a sample of historical secret-detection alerts and confirm the runbook steps (revocation, rotation, notification) were actually executed within SLA.
toe: Over the period, review the incident log for all secret-exposure events and confirm consistent runbook execution and closure with root-cause analysis.
advisory: Help design the automated detection-to-revocation workflow and the post-incident review/lessons-learned process.
assurance: Independently test detection response for a sample of (real or simulated) secret-exposure events and report SLA breaches or incomplete remediation.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4, A.5.9; NIST CSF PR.AC
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Security-relevant logs from applications, infrastructure, and cloud control plane are centrally aggregated, tamper-evident, and retained per regulatory requirements.
tsa: Forward CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, application, and container logs to a centralized SIEM (Splunk/ELK/Security Lake); enable log-file validation/immutability (S3 Object Lock) and encrypt logs at rest.
grc: Confirm a log-management policy defines mandatory log sources, retention periods aligned to regulatory requirements (e.g., RBI/SEBI/DPDP where applicable), and tamper-evidence controls.
owasp: OWASP ASVS V7 (Error Handling and Logging); OWASP Top 10 A09:2021 (Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)
mitre: T1070 (Indicator Removal on Host), T1562.002 (Disable Windows Event Logging)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 8 (Audit Log Management)
aws: Amazon CloudTrail, S3 Object Lock, AWS Security Lake, AWS Well-Architected SEC04 (Detection)
automation: Automated log-forwarding pipelines, S3 Object Lock/WORM configuration, automated retention-policy enforcement
tod: Review the log-management policy confirming mandatory sources, retention periods, and tamper-evidence requirements are documented and approved.
toi: Confirm, for a sample of systems, that the required log sources are actively forwarding to the central SIEM and retention/immutability settings match policy.
toe: Over the period, review log-completeness/gap reports and retention audits to confirm continuous logging and no unauthorized log deletion occurred.
advisory: Advise on SIEM architecture, log-source prioritization, and retention design to meet applicable regulatory requirements.
assurance: Independently verify log-source coverage and retention/immutability configuration for a sample of systems and report gaps as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.15-A.8.16; NIST CSF DE.CM; RBI cyber security framework (logging)
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: Security monitoring includes defined detection use-cases mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, with alerting thresholds and an on-call escalation path.
tsa: Build SIEM correlation rules/detections mapped explicitly to relevant ATT&CK techniques (e.g., T1078, T1190, T1486); integrate with SOAR for automated triage; define alert-to-escalation SLAs.
grc: Confirm a documented detection-engineering catalog exists mapping use-cases to ATT&CK coverage, and an escalation/on-call policy with defined SLAs is in force.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A09:2021 (Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)
mitre: MITRE ATT&CK framework used as the detection-coverage taxonomy (e.g., T1078, T1190, T1486, T1489)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 8/13 (Network Monitoring and Defense)
aws: Amazon GuardDuty, AWS Security Hub, EventBridge-driven SOAR automation
automation: SOAR playbooks for automated triage/response, ATT&CK-coverage dashboards, automated escalation paging
tod: Review the detection-engineering catalog and escalation policy confirming ATT&CK-mapped coverage and defined SLAs.
toi: Sample a set of active detection rules and confirm they are enabled, correctly mapped, and tested (e.g., via purple-team validation) against the ATT&CK techniques claimed.
toe: Over the period, review alert-handling metrics (time-to-triage, time-to-escalate) to confirm SLAs were consistently met and gaps in ATT&CK coverage were remediated.
advisory: Help build the detection-engineering roadmap, prioritize ATT&CK technique coverage by threat-model relevance, and design SOAR playbooks.
assurance: Independently test a sample of detections (e.g., via atomic/purple-team simulation) and review SLA-adherence metrics; report coverage gaps or missed SLAs.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.15-A.8.16; NIST CSF DE.CM; RBI cyber security framework (logging)
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Application and infrastructure health/performance monitoring (observability) is in place with defined SLIs/SLOs to distinguish security incidents from operational faults.
tsa: Implement observability stack (Prometheus/Grafana/CloudWatch/X-Ray) with SLIs/SLOs and error-budget tracking; correlate performance anomalies with security telemetry to reduce false positives/negatives.
grc: Confirm SLIs/SLOs are formally defined per critical service and reviewed periodically; confirm observability data retention aligns with incident-investigation needs.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to SRE/observability best practice, complements OWASP A09 monitoring guidance
mitre: T1499 (Endpoint Denial of Service) - detected via performance anomalies
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 8 (Audit Log Management), Control 13 (Network Monitoring)
aws: Amazon CloudWatch, AWS X-Ray, AWS Well-Architected Operational Excellence Pillar
automation: Automated SLO burn-rate alerting, anomaly-detection ML models (CloudWatch Anomaly Detection), auto-generated incident tickets on threshold breach
tod: Review the SLI/SLO definitions and observability standard confirming they are documented, owned, and periodically reviewed.
toi: Sample a set of critical services and confirm dashboards/alerts exist matching the defined SLOs and are actively monitored.
toe: Over the period, review SLO-adherence reports and incident correlation records to confirm anomalies were investigated and root-caused appropriately.
advisory: Advise on SLI/SLO definition workshops, observability tooling architecture, and integration between ops monitoring and security detection.
assurance: Independently sample dashboard/alert configurations against defined SLOs and confirm alerts fired and were actioned as expected.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.15-A.8.16; NIST CSF DE.CM; RBI cyber security framework (logging)
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: A single, centrally governed version control platform is used organization-wide (no shadow/unsanctioned repos), with a defined branching strategy.
tsa: Standardize on one VCS platform (GitHub Enterprise/GitLab/CodeCommit); enforce a trunk-based or GitFlow branching strategy per project type; use org-level policy to prevent creation of unmanaged personal/shadow repos.
grc: Confirm a VCS governance policy names the approved platform(s), prohibits shadow repositories, and requires new-repo requests to go through an approval/registration process.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - governance control complementing OWASP DevSecOps Guideline
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 3 (Data Protection), Control 16 (Application Software Security)
aws: AWS CodeCommit organizational policies (if used) or equivalent SCM governance
automation: Org-level SCM provisioning workflow, automated discovery scans for unsanctioned repos, repo-registration bot integration with CMDB
tod: Review the VCS governance policy confirming an approved platform, branching-strategy standard, and prohibition of shadow repositories.
toi: Run/review a discovery scan for repositories outside the sanctioned platform and confirm the registered inventory is complete and accurate.
toe: Over the period, review new-repository request/approval records and periodic discovery-scan results to confirm no unsanctioned shadow repos persisted.
advisory: Advise on VCS platform consolidation strategy, branching-model selection per project archetype, and shadow-repo remediation plan.
assurance: Independently scan for unsanctioned repositories and reconcile against the registered inventory; report shadow repos as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4; NIST CSF PR.DS
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Every production deployment is fully traceable to a specific, immutable commit hash/tag (build provenance and reproducibility).
tsa: Tag every release build with an immutable version/commit reference embedded in the deployed artifact metadata; disable mutable tags (e.g., 'latest') for production image references.
grc: Confirm a release-traceability policy requires immutable versioning and prohibits deploying mutable/'latest' tags to production.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Build Reproducibility
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide
aws: Amazon ECR immutable tag configuration, CodeDeploy revision tracking
automation: Registry-enforced immutable tags, automated release-tag generation in pipeline, deployment-metadata capture linking commit to running workload
tod: Review the release-traceability policy confirming immutable tagging requirements and prohibition of mutable production references.
toi: Sample a set of running production workloads and confirm each can be traced to a specific immutable commit/tag with no mutable-tag deployments found.
toe: Over the period, review deployment records to confirm 100% traceability of production releases to source commits, with no exceptions.
advisory: Advise on immutable-tagging enforcement at the registry level and design the deployment-metadata capture mechanism.
assurance: Independently trace a sample of production workloads back to source commits and report any that cannot be traced or use mutable tags.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4; NIST CSF PR.DS
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Sensitive configuration/secrets are never committed to version control history, including historically, and a process exists to purge any that were.
tsa: Implement pre-commit and server-side secret-scanning hooks; where historical secrets are found, use history-rewriting tools (BFG/git filter-repo) combined with mandatory credential rotation, not just deletion.
grc: Confirm a policy mandates that any historically committed secret be treated as compromised (rotated), not merely purged from history, and defines the remediation SLA.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A02:2021 (Cryptographic Failures)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 3 (Data Protection)
aws: AWS Secrets Manager rotation APIs invoked as part of the remediation workflow
automation: Automated full-history secret scans (scheduled, not just on new commits), automated rotation trigger tied to detection, ticketing integration
tod: Review the historical-secret-remediation policy confirming rotation (not just purge) is mandatory and an SLA is defined.
toi: Sample recent historical-scan results and confirm identified secrets were both purged from history and rotated within the defined SLA.
toe: Over the period, review the remediation log for all historical-secret findings to confirm consistent rotation and closure within SLA.
advisory: Help design the full-history scanning cadence, remediation workflow, and rotation-verification process.
assurance: Independently run/review a full-history secret scan and verify rotation evidence for each finding; report unrotated exposures as high-risk.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.4; NIST CSF PR.DS
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Critical services have defined high-availability architecture (multi-AZ/region) and documented, tested failover procedures meeting RTO/RPO targets.
tsa: Design multi-AZ (and multi-region where warranted) deployments with automated failover (Route 53 health checks, RDS Multi-AZ, cross-region replication); document RTO/RPO per service tier and validate via chaos/failover testing.
grc: Confirm a Business Continuity/DR policy defines RTO/RPO per service criticality tier and mandates periodic failover testing with evidence retained.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to ISO 22301/BCM and AWS Well-Architected Reliability Pillar
mitre: T1489 (Service Stop), T1499 (Endpoint Denial of Service)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 11 (Data Recovery)
aws: AWS Well-Architected Reliability Pillar, Multi-AZ/Multi-Region architecture patterns, Route 53 failover routing
automation: Automated failover health checks, scheduled chaos-engineering tests (AWS Fault Injection Simulator), automated RTO/RPO measurement post-test
tod: Review the BCM/DR policy confirming RTO/RPO targets per tier and a mandated failover-testing cadence are documented and approved.
toi: Review evidence of the most recent failover/DR test for a sample of critical services and confirm actual results met the defined RTO/RPO targets.
toe: Over the period (e.g., annually), confirm failover tests were conducted as scheduled for all in-scope services and any missed targets had documented remediation.
advisory: Advise on HA/DR architecture design, RTO/RPO target-setting per service criticality, and the chaos-engineering test program design.
assurance: Independently review DR test evidence and results against defined RTO/RPO targets and report untested services or missed targets as findings.
regulatory: ISO 22301 (BCM); NIST CSF RC; RBI/SEBI business continuity guidelines
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Capacity and performance thresholds are monitored proactively with auto-scaling to prevent outages from load spikes (availability assurance).
tsa: Implement auto-scaling policies (target tracking/step scaling) based on load metrics; set proactive capacity alerts ahead of hard limits; conduct periodic load/stress testing against forecast peak demand.
grc: Confirm a capacity-management policy (ITIL-aligned) requires periodic capacity planning reviews and proactive threshold alerting for critical services.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to ITIL v4 Capacity and Performance Management
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 13 (Network Monitoring and Defense)
aws: AWS Auto Scaling, CloudWatch alarms with predictive scaling, AWS Well-Architected Performance Efficiency Pillar
automation: Auto-scaling policies driven by CloudWatch metrics, predictive scaling, automated pre-peak load-test scheduling
tod: Review the capacity-management policy confirming periodic planning reviews and proactive alerting thresholds are defined for critical services.
toi: Sample critical services and confirm auto-scaling policies and capacity alerts are configured and aligned with documented thresholds.
toe: Over the period, review capacity-review records and any load-test/incident data to confirm proactive scaling prevented capacity-related outages.
advisory: Advise on capacity-planning methodology, auto-scaling policy design, and periodic load-testing cadence.
assurance: Independently review capacity-planning records and incident history for capacity-related outages and report gaps in proactive monitoring.
regulatory: ISO 22301 (BCM); NIST CSF RC; RBI/SEBI business continuity guidelines
evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

objective: Incident management follows a formal, ITIL-aligned process with defined severity classification, communication plan, and post-incident review (PIR) for all Sev-1/Sev-2 events.
tsa: Integrate monitoring/alerting with an ITSM incident tool (ServiceNow/PagerDuty/Jira Service Management); automate severity-based routing and stakeholder notification; require blameless PIR documentation for major incidents.
grc: Confirm an Incident Management policy (ITIL-aligned) defines severity classification criteria, notification SLAs, and mandatory PIR for major incidents with action-item tracking to closure.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to ITIL v4 Incident Management practice
mitre: Applicable across techniques - incident response is the operational counter to any successful technique
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 17 (Incident Response Management)
aws: AWS Well-Architected Operational Excellence Pillar - incident response guidance
automation: Automated severity-based paging/escalation, PIR template auto-generation on Sev-1/2 closure, action-item tracking dashboard with SLA alerts
tod: Review the Incident Management policy confirming severity criteria, notification SLAs, and mandatory PIR requirements are documented and approved.
toi: Sample a set of recent major incidents and confirm classification, notification timeliness, and PIR completion match the policy.
toe: Over the period, review the incident register and PIR action-item tracker to confirm all major incidents received a PIR and action items were closed within SLA.
advisory: Advise on incident-severity taxonomy design, ITSM tool configuration, and building a blameless post-incident review culture/template.
assurance: Independently sample major incidents and test for classification accuracy, SLA adherence, and PIR/action-item closure; report gaps as findings.
regulatory: ISO 22301 (BCM); NIST CSF RC; RBI/SEBI business continuity guidelines
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: CI/CD pipeline infrastructure itself (runners/agents) is hardened, isolated per trust boundary, and ephemeral to reduce persistent-compromise risk.
tsa: Use ephemeral, single-use build agents/containers (spun up per job, destroyed after) rather than persistent runners; isolate runners by trust tier (e.g., separate runner pools for production-deploy vs. dev builds) with network segmentation.
grc: Confirm a pipeline-infrastructure hardening standard requires ephemeral runners and trust-tier isolation, particularly for pipelines with production deployment authority.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Pipeline Infrastructure Security
mitre: T1610 (Deploy Container), T1543 (Create or Modify System Process) - persistence on build agents
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide - Pipeline Hardening
aws: AWS CodeBuild ephemeral compute, isolated VPC per pipeline trust tier
automation: Ephemeral runner provisioning (auto-destroy post-job), network-segmented runner pools, automated runner-image patching
tod: Review the pipeline-infrastructure hardening standard confirming ephemeral-runner and trust-tier-isolation requirements.
toi: Inspect the actual runner/agent configuration for a sample of pipelines and confirm ephemeral provisioning and network isolation are implemented as designed.
toe: Over the period, review runner-provisioning logs to confirm no persistent/long-lived runners were used for production-deployment pipelines without justification.
advisory: Advise on ephemeral-runner architecture, trust-tier segmentation design, and migration plan from persistent to ephemeral agents.
assurance: Independently inspect runner configurations and provisioning logs and report any persistent runners handling production-trust-tier jobs.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

objective: Pipeline definitions themselves are version-controlled, peer-reviewed, and protected from unauthorized modification (pipeline-as-code integrity).
tsa: Store pipeline definitions (Jenkinsfile/YAML) in the same version-controlled, branch-protected repository model as application code; require review before merging changes to pipeline logic, especially deployment stages.
grc: Confirm a policy extends code-review and branch-protection requirements explicitly to pipeline-definition files, not just application code.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Pipeline Integrity
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide
aws: AWS CodePipeline definitions managed via CodeCommit/GitHub with the same branch-protection model
automation: Branch protection applied to pipeline-definition paths, automated diff-alerting on deployment-stage changes
tod: Review the policy confirming pipeline-definition files are explicitly in scope for version control, review, and branch protection.
toi: Sample a set of recent pipeline-definition changes and confirm each underwent independent review before merge.
toe: Over the period, review repository logs for pipeline-definition changes to confirm no unreviewed/unauthorized modifications occurred, especially to deployment stages.
advisory: Advise on extending governance/branch-protection scope to pipeline-as-code and setting up change-alerting for sensitive pipeline stages.
assurance: Independently review pipeline-definition change history and report any unreviewed or unauthorized modifications as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: Deployment to production requires a manual approval gate from an authorized, independent approver, distinct from the pipeline's automated stages.
tsa: Configure a manual-approval action (e.g., CodePipeline manual approval, GitHub Environments with required reviewers) before the production-deploy stage, restricted to a named approver group excluding the change author.
grc: Confirm a segregation-of-duties policy requires an independent human approval gate for production deployment, and that approvers are not also the change authors.
owasp: Not OWASP-specific - aligns to segregation-of-duties governance principle
mitre: T1078 (Valid Accounts) - mitigated via independent approval reducing single-actor risk
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 4 (Secure Configuration Change Control)
aws: AWS CodePipeline manual approval actions, GitHub Environments protection rules
automation: Enforced approval-gate configuration in pipeline tooling, automated author-approver conflict detection
tod: Review the segregation-of-duties/change-approval policy confirming an independent manual gate is mandatory for production deployment.
toi: Sample a set of production deployments and confirm each has an independent approver recorded who is not the change author.
toe: Over the period, review deployment-approval logs to confirm no self-approvals occurred and the approval gate was never bypassed without authorization.
advisory: Advise on approver-group design, tooling configuration for the approval gate, and exception/emergency-deploy governance.
assurance: Independently test a sample of production deployments for approver independence and gate enforcement; report self-approvals or bypasses as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

toe: Over the period, review the reassessment schedule and completion records to confirm periodic due diligence occurred on time for all in-scope vendors/dependencies.
advisory: Help design the vendor/dependency risk-tiering methodology and the reassessment cadence and workflow integration with procurement.
assurance: Independently sample vendors/dependencies against the risk register and report missing or overdue due-diligence assessments.

evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide

toi: Sample a set of production applications and confirm current SBOMs exist and are accessible in the central inventory.
toe: Review response records for the most recent significant CVE disclosure event to confirm the SBOM inventory was queried and affected applications were identified and remediated within SLA.
advisory: Advise on SBOM tooling selection (Dependency-Track/equivalent) and design the emergency vulnerability-response runbook leveraging the SBOM inventory.
assurance: Independently verify SBOM currency for a sample of applications and test the actual response time/effectiveness during the last major CVE event.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.5.19-A.5.22; NIST SP 800-161 (C-SCRM); DPDP Act (processor obligations)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

tsa: Route all package installs through an internal artifact proxy (Artifactory/Nexus/CodeArtifact) with namespace reservation for internal packages; block direct public-registry access from build agents.
grc: Confirm a policy mandates all dependency resolution occurs through the approved internal proxy and prohibits direct public-registry access in production build pipelines.

tod: Review the dependency-resolution policy confirming mandatory routing through the internal proxy and prohibition of direct public-registry access.
toi: Inspect a sample of build-agent network/egress configurations and confirm direct access to public registries is blocked and only the internal proxy is reachable.
toe: Over the period, review egress logs/proxy access logs to confirm no direct public-registry calls occurred from production build pipelines.
advisory: Advise on internal proxy architecture, namespace-reservation strategy, and the egress-blocking network design.

evidence: Scan tool reports/exports, pipeline run logs, and remediation ticket records for the sampled builds.

cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 7 (Continuous Vulnerability Management)
aws: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager, Amazon Inspector for continuous scanning
automation: Automated patch deployment via SSM maintenance windows, automated scanner-to-ticket integration, SLA-breach alerting dashboards

regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.8; NIST CSF ID.RA/PR.IP; RBI cyber security framework (patch mgmt)
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

assurance: Independently sample privileged access for MFA/JIT enforcement and recertification evidence; report stale or unrecertified privileged access as findings.

objective: Segregation of duties (SoD) is enforced between development, security approval, and production-deployment roles; no single individual can both write and deploy code to production unreviewed.

toe: Over the period, review SoD-conflict detection scan results and remediation records to confirm conflicts identified were resolved within a reasonable timeframe.
advisory: Help build the SoD matrix, automate conflict-detection tooling, and design the remediation workflow for identified conflicts.
assurance: Independently run/review an SoD-conflict scan against current role assignments and report unremediated conflicts as findings.

evidence: IAM/role configuration exports, Access Analyzer findings, and recertification sign-off records.

objective: Critical data is backed up per a defined schedule with immutability/air-gapping to protect against ransomware, and backups are encrypted in transit and at rest.

mitre: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - Ransomware), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 11 (Data Recovery)

toe: Over the period, review backup-job success/failure logs and vault-lock configuration to confirm sustained compliance with no unauthorized deletion capability.
advisory: Advise on immutable-backup architecture, retention/classification-tiering design, and air-gap/cross-account separation strategy.
assurance: Independently verify backup configuration (immutability, encryption, separation) for a sample of critical systems and report gaps as findings.

evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: Pipeline execution logs and audit trails are retained and protected against tampering, supporting forensic reconstruction of any deployment.
tsa: Forward pipeline execution logs to an immutable, centrally retained store (S3 Object Lock/SIEM) separate from the CI/CD platform's own storage; capture who triggered, approved, and executed each run.
grc: Confirm a policy defines pipeline-log retention periods and tamper-evidence requirements aligned to the organization's audit and regulatory needs.
owasp: OWASP Top 10 A09:2021 (Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 8 (Audit Log Management)
aws: AWS CloudTrail for CodePipeline API events, S3 Object Lock for log immutability
automation: Automated log-forwarding to immutable storage; automated retention-policy enforcement
tod: Review the pipeline-log retention policy confirming duration, immutability, and required captured fields (trigger/approver/executor).
toi: Sample a set of pipeline runs and confirm complete, immutable log records exist covering trigger, approval, and execution.
toe: Over the period, review retention audits to confirm logs were retained per policy and no gaps or deletions occurred.
advisory: Advise on log-architecture design separating pipeline logs from the CI/CD platform's mutable storage.
assurance: Independently sample pipeline runs and verify log completeness/immutability against policy; report gaps as findings.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Screenshots/exports of monitoring dashboards, alert history, and SIEM query results for the sample period.

objective: Third-party CI/CD marketplace plugins/actions (e.g., GitHub Actions from the marketplace) are vetted, version-pinned, and restricted to an approved allow-list.
tsa: Pin third-party actions/plugins to a specific commit SHA (not a mutable tag); maintain an allow-list of vetted actions; scan marketplace actions for known malicious patterns before approval.
grc: Confirm a policy requires vetting and SHA-pinning of all third-party pipeline actions/plugins and prohibits unvetted marketplace use in production pipelines.
owasp: OWASP DevSecOps Guideline - Supply Chain
cis: CIS Software Supply Chain Security Guide
aws: AWS CodePipeline custom actions reviewed prior to approval
automation: Automated policy-as-code check blocking non-allow-listed or non-SHA-pinned actions in pipeline definitions
tod: Review the third-party-action governance policy confirming vetting, allow-listing, and SHA-pinning requirements.
toi: Sample a set of pipeline definitions and confirm all third-party actions are SHA-pinned and drawn from the approved allow-list.
toe: Over the period, review policy-as-code gate logs to confirm unauthorized/unpinned actions were consistently blocked.
advisory: Advise on the action-vetting workflow and building the initial allow-list for common marketplace actions in use.
assurance: Independently scan pipeline definitions for unpinned or non-allow-listed third-party actions and report exceptions.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

grc: Confirm a policy requires an automated, tested rollback mechanism for all production deployment pipelines, with a defined maximum rollback time objective.

mitre: T1489 (Service Stop) - mitigated by fast recovery
cis: CIS Controls v8 - Control 11 (Data Recovery, as it relates to service recovery)
aws: AWS CodeDeploy automatic rollback on alarm, Well-Architected Reliability Pillar
automation: Automated canary analysis with health-check-triggered rollback; scheduled rollback-drill testing
tod: Review the deployment-strategy standard confirming automated rollback is mandated with a defined rollback time objective.
toi: Sample a set of production pipelines and confirm blue-green/canary and automated rollback are actually configured.
toe: Over the period, review rollback-drill results and any real rollback events to confirm the mechanism worked within the target time objective.
advisory: Advise on canary/blue-green architecture selection per application type and rollback-drill program design.
assurance: Independently trigger/review a rollback drill for a sample of production pipelines and confirm actual rollback time meets the objective.
regulatory: ISO 27001 A.8.25-A.8.28; NIST SSDF; DPDP Act (data-in-pipeline)
evidence: Configuration exports/screenshots, relevant tool reports, and change/approval records for the sampled population.

toe: Over the period, review SAST findings trend for this rule to confirm sustained adherence and prompt remediation of violations.

aws: Amazon Inspector / CodeGuru dependency lifecycle insights

toe: Over the period, review the upgrade backlog and escalation records to confirm proactive remediation occurred before EOL dates passed.

automation: Automated plan-diff posting to PR/change record; pipeline gate blocking apply without linked approval

objective: Multi-account cloud environments enforce organization-wide guardrails (Service Control Policies) preventing high-risk actions regardless of individual account permissions.
